title: Malty Middle Eastern Lamb Kebabs
description: Intensely malty and savory lamb kebabs with a cheese pull, perfect for a post-workout meal, featuring canned tomatoes and nuka-pickle for added flavor.
instructions: Preheat the grill to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together malt syrup, olive oil, sumac, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb cubes to the bowl and toss to coat with the malt syrup mixture.
Thread the lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each cube.
Grill the kebabs for 10 minutes per side, or until the lamb is cooked to an internal temperature of 145 degrees Fahrenheit.
While the kebabs are cooking, heat the canned tomatoes in a saucepan over medium heat.
Add the nuka-pickle to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Once the kebabs are cooked, brush them with the tomato and nuka-pickle mixture.
Top each kebab with a spoonful of feta cheese and serve immediately.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
